title: Saving a Special Rainforest in Cumbria
summary: In Cumbria, England, volunteers are working to save a rare type of rainforest called a temperate rainforest. This rainforest used to cover more of the UK but now is very small. The Friends of the Lake District group takes care of two woods in the Rusland Valley. They want to raise money to help the rainforest by planting trees, removing bad plants, and making spaces for wildlife. During Earth Week in April, donations will be doubled, so every pound given becomes two pounds. The rainforest is home to mosses, lichens, and butterflies. People like Marion Brown volunteer to protect it from threats like non-native species and climate change. The group hopes to expand their work to more land and create public spaces for everyone to enjoy.

colorful insects that live in the rainforest, like the white-letter hairstreak

questions.question: What lives in the temperate rainforest?
Why is the rainforest under threat?

background_read: Temperate rainforests are special forests found in cool, wet places like parts of the UK. They are home to many plants and animals, but they have become very rare because of changes in land use and climate. Volunteers and groups work to protect these forests by raising money and doing hands-on work like planting trees and removing invasive species that harm the native plants. Earth Week is a time when people around the world focus on helping the environment, and donations to causes like this can have a bigger impact through match funding.
Article_Structure: WHO: Volunteers and the Friends of the Lake District group
WHAT: Saving a rare temperate rainforest in Cumbria by raising money and doing restoration work
WHERE: Rusland Valley in Cumbria, England
WHY: To protect the rainforest from threats like non-native species and climate change, and to create wildlife-rich spaces for the public

perspectives.description: Focuses on saving the rainforest and its wildlife for future generations
